text: IRGC fired 3 ballistic missile waves at Israel within one hour this morning. Cluster munitions struck Bnei Brak — 16+ wounded, 11-year-old girl critical at Sheba Medical Center. Fourth wave intercepted later. Despite Hegseth saying 'lowest enemy missile fire,' Iran clearly retains strike capability.
verificationNote: Times of Israel, CGTN confirmed casualty figures and strike details.

text: Mobarakeh Steel Complex in Isfahan struck for second time. Iran's largest steel plant. Massive fireballs overnight geolocated by NBC News. Meanwhile IDF hit Shahid Haqqani Port (Bandar Abbas), Bushehr met facility, residential complex in Malard, and Grand Hosseinieh of Zanjan mosque. Systematic industrial/infrastructure destruction campaign continues.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera liveblog, Euronews, JNS confirmed.

text: Iranian cruise missile hits QatarEnergy oil tanker Aqua 1 just 17nm from Ras Laffan. Qatar intercepted 2 of 3 missiles. 21 crew evacuated safely. Meanwhile Iranian drone set Kuwait airport fuel depots ablaze. Gulf states under sustained bombardment — Bahrain fire, 1 killed in Fujairah from drone debris.
verificationNote: Al Arabiya, Kuwait Times, The National confirmed.

text: Araghchi drops bombshell in Al Jazeera interview: Iran prepared for 6 months of war, Russia 'helping in many directions,' demands Lebanon included in any ceasefire. Meanwhile Trump says no deal needed, leaving in 2-3 weeks. WSJ: willing to end without reopening Hormuz. Huge gap between US exit talk and Iran's defiance.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera interview verified. NPR, WaPo confirmed Trump statements.

text: Hegseth: 'upcoming days decisive,' Iran defense industrial base near complete destruction. CENTCOM claims 75% of Iranian missile launchers destroyed. But today's 3-wave missile attack on Israel and sustained Gulf bombardment suggest Iran still has significant offensive capability. Netanyahu: 'beyond the halfway point.'
verificationNote: war.gov, CBS News, Times of Israel confirmed.

text: Oil seesawing: Brent $105.27 (volatile $101-$108 range), WTI $102.92. Markets torn between Trump exit talk and continued escalation. S&P rallied 2.91% March 31. Analyst Neil Crosby: 'A ceasefire does not equal supply restoration. Physical relief is weeks-to-months away.' Goldman: Hormuz shock 17x larger than Russia disruption.
verificationNote: CNBC confirmed oil prices. Goldman Sachs analysis cited by multiple outlets.

text: Day 33 AM summary (4:32 AM PDT): (1) IRGC 3 missile waves at Israel, 16 wounded; (2) Mobarakeh Steel re-struck; (3) IDF Tehran infrastructure wave; (4) Aqua 1 tanker hit off Qatar; (5) Kuwait airport fuel fire; (6) 1 killed Fujairah; (7) Trump: 2-3 weeks, no deal needed; (8) Araghchi: 6 months ready, Russia helping; (9) Hegseth: decisive days; (10) Oil Brent $105.27/WTI $102.92.
verificationNote: Compiled from CNBC, Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, Al Arabiya, Kuwait Times, NPR, WaPo.
